decks.notes: The main focus of this deck is to disrupt the opponent's resources and control the game through land destruction and direct damage spells. By using cards like Avalanche Riders, Stone Rain, and Pillage, the deck aims to keep the opponent on the back foot by limiting their mana sources. Grim Lavamancer and Lightning Bolt provide efficient removal options, while Black Vise and Cursed Scroll offer additional damage output. The inclusion of cards like Masticore and Ghitu Encampment provide versatile threats to close out the game. The deck's strategy revolves around denying the opponent's resources while maintaining control of the battlefield.

The main focus of this deck is to disrupt the opponent's strategy while building up a strong board presence with creatures like Ravenous Baloth and Spiritmonger. Cards like Duress and Cabal Therapy help to control the opponent's hand, allowing for key removal with spells like Smother and Pernicious Deed. Recurring Nightmare provides additional creature recursion options, while Call of the Herd and Wall of Blossoms provide card advantage and board presence. Overall, the deck aims to outlast the opponent by controlling the game and outvaluing them with efficient creatures and disruption.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by utilizing counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage to maintain board control. It aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while generating incremental advantage through efficient card draw and synergy between land and creature cards. The deck focuses on efficient mana usage and resource management to outlast the opponent and ultimately win through incremental advantage and board control.

The main focus of this mono black control deck is to aggressively disrupt the opponent's hand and board with discard spells like Duress and Cabal Therapy, while using cards like Ravenous Rats and The Rack to apply pressure and deplete the opponent's resources. Pox and Innocent Blood are used to further disrupt the board and maintain control, with Cursed Scroll providing additional removal. The deck aims to grind out the opponent through attrition and resource denial, while Mishra's Factory and Wasteland provide utility and additional pressure.

The deck focuses on leveraging the synergy and speed of goblin creatures to overwhelm the opponent with fast, aggressive plays. By using cards like Goblin Lackey and Goblin Warchief to quickly get creatures onto the battlefield, along with powerful cards like Siege-Gang Commander and Goblin Piledriver to deal heavy damage, the deck aims to maintain pressure on the opponent and finish the game before they have a chance to stabilize. Supporting cards like Gempalm Incinerator and Naturalize help to remove threats or obstacles that could hinder the goblin's assault.

The deck focuses on controlling the game by utilizing countermagic and removal spells while also utilizing the powerful Oath of Druids card to cheat large creatures into play. The deck includes ways to protect itself from threats, such as Seal of Cleansing and Misdirection, while also generating card advantage through cards like Fact or Fiction and Sylvan Library. The mana base is built to provide a variety of colors and a mix of utility lands to support the overall game plan of controlling the board and eventually overpowering opponents with powerful creatures.

The main idea of this aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with fast and efficient creatures and burn spells. By playing creatures like Jackal Pup and Mogg Fanatic early on, the deck aims to deal damage quickly while also using burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Incinerate to finish off the opponent. Key cards like Fireblast and Price of Progress can deliver a final blow, while cards like Cursed Scroll and Sulfuric Vortex provide additional damage over time. The deck aims to swiftly reduce the opponent's life total and secure a fast victory.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by using removal spells, discard spells, and versatile creatures. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy with cards like Duress and Cabal Therapy, while also generating card advantage with cards like Sylvan Library and Skeletal Scrying. The Rock archetype emphasizes a balanced mix of efficient removal, hand disruption, and powerful threats to outvalue the opponent and control the board. The strategy is to grind down the opponent's resources and outlast them in a battle of attrition.

The main focus of this deck is to take advantage of the Enchantress cards to draw cards whenever you cast an enchantment. By using cards like Elephant Grass and Solitary Confinement to protect yourself while building up your enchantments, you can quickly assemble a powerful board state. The key combo involves using Opalescence to turn your enchantments into creatures, then using Parallax Wave to exile your opponent's creatures permanently. With plenty of mana acceleration from Wild Growth and Exploration, you can overwhelm your opponent with a powerful enchantment-based army.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive Goblin tribal strategy, utilizing creatures like Goblin Lackey and Goblin Piledriver to quickly deal damage. The deck also includes cards like Goblin Matron and Goblin Ringleader to fetch more Goblins and maintain card advantage. The inclusion of cards like Gempalm Incinerator and Siege-Gang Commander helps to control the board and deal with threats. Overall, the deck aims to apply constant pressure and win the game before the opponent can effectively respond.

The core idea behind this Aggro - Machine Head deck is to quickly deploy threats like Grim Lavamancer, Hypnotic Specter, and Nantuko Shade, while disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Duress and Cabal Therapy. The deck aims to maintain pressure on the opponent with efficient creatures and burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Terminate, while utilizing synergy with cards like Cursed Scroll and Skittering Skirge to overwhelm and control the game. With a mix of aggressive creatures and disruptive spells, this deck aims to take control of the battlefield and secure a quick victory.

The deck focuses on using aggressive creatures like Grim Lavamancer, Ravenous Rats, and Hypnotic Specter to quickly deplete the opponent's resources and deal damage. It also includes disruption cards like Duress and Cabal Therapy to disrupt the opponent's strategy. Additionally, burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Terminate are included for removal and additional damage. The deck utilizes cards like The Rack and Phyrexian Arena for card advantage and additional pressure. Overall, the deck aims to apply consistent pressure and disrupt the opponent's game plan to secure a quick victory.

The main focus of this Mono Black Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's hand and board with cards like Duress, Cabal Therapy, and Withered Wretch, while applying pressure with efficient beaters like Hypnotic Specter and Mishra's Factory. The deck aims to control the game through discard spells, removal, and card advantage from The Rack, ultimately grinding out the opponent's resources and winning through attrition. The inclusion of disruptive lands like Wasteland and utility cards like Phyrexian Furnace and Cursed Scroll further enhances the deck's ability to control the game and come out on top.

The main concept of this deck is to disrupt the opponent's mana base and control the game with resource denial cards like Stone Rain and Wasteland, while also utilizing Oath of Druids to cheat out powerful creatures like Terravore. The deck aims to slow down the opponent's game plan and outvalue them with cards like Sylvan Library and Mox Diamond. Winter's Grasp and Pyroclasm provide additional removal options, while Naturalize deals with problematic artifacts or enchantments. The mana base is designed to support the control elements of the deck while also providing access to all necessary colors.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly fill up the graveyard with cards that have madness and reach threshold, allowing for efficient and aggressive plays. By combining madness cards like Basking Rootwalla and Circular Logic with threshold cards like Arrogant Wurm and Wonder, the deck can overwhelm opponents with powerful creatures and spells. The deck also includes card draw and control elements like Merfolk Looter and Counterspell to help maintain board presence and disrupt the opponent's strategy.

This deck focuses on quickly playing low-cost, aggressive white creatures to overwhelm the opponent in combat. It includes protection and disruption spells to clear the way for the creatures to attack. Armageddon and Wasteland are utilized to disrupt the opponent's mana base and slow down their game plan. The deck also includes lifegain and board control elements to help maintain its aggressive strategy and outlast the opponent. The overall goal is to maintain a fast and aggressive tempo while controlling the board and wearing down the opponent's defenses.
